title: andromeda paniculata var. pubescens
intro: Andromeda paniculata var. pubescens, also known as the hairy bog-rosemary, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. This plant is native to the eastern Himalayas and southern China, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The hairy bog-rosemary has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. It can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to promote health and well-being.
